title: 300,000 protesters flood Seoul streets in largest demonstrations since 2024 impeachment
summary: Approximately 300,000 demonstrators gathered in Seoul protesting President Yoon's labor crackdown amid a 4% unemployment spike. The May Day protests represent the largest mass demonstrations since the 2024 impeachment crisis.
contents:
# 300,000 Protesters March in Seoul Against Yoon Administration

## Scale and Context
Approximately 300,000 demonstrators filled Seoul streets on May 1, 2026, making this the largest mass protest since the 2024 impeachment crisis. The May Day demonstrations targeted President Yoon's labor policies and economic management.

## Driving Factors
- Unemployment rate spiked to 4%, the highest in recent years
- Government labor crackdown drew widespread criticism
- May Day (International Workers' Day) amplified labor movement mobilization
- Economic grievances merged with political opposition to Yoon administration

## Protest Characteristics
Organizers coordinated the demonstrations through labor unions and civic organizations. Participants marched through central Seoul, concentrating around government buildings and major intersections. Reports indicate a largely peaceful gathering with heightened police presence.

## Regional Significance
South Korea's political instability coincides with heightened security concerns on the Korean Peninsula. Analysts monitor whether domestic turmoil affects the US-ROK alliance and military readiness.

## International Pattern
May Day protests also erupted across 50+ countries, including France (100,000+), India (250,000), and Brazil (80,000), reflecting global labor and economic tensions.

## Tags
south-korea, seoul, protests, may-day, labor, yoon-administration, political-crisis
